content.title: Case Study: Tourism New Zealand website

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: New Zealand is a small country of four million inhabitants, a long-haul flight from all the major tourist-generating markets of the world. Tourism currently makes up 9% of the country's gross domestic product, and is the country's largest export sector. Unlike other export sectors, which make products and then sell them overseas, tourism brings its customers to New Zealand. The product is the country itself - the people, the places and the experiences. In 1999, Tourism New Zealand launched a campaign to communicate a new brand position to the world. The campaign focused on New Zealand's scenic beauty, exhilarating outdoor activities and authentic Maori culture, and it made New Zealand one of the strongest national brands in the world.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: A key feature of the campaign was the website www.newzealand.com, which provided potential visitors to New Zealand with a single gateway to everything the destination had to offer. The heart of the website was a database of tourism service operators, both those based in New Zealand and those based abroad which offered tourism services to the country. Any tourism-related business could be listed by filling in a simple form. This meant that even the smallest bed and breakfast address or specialist activity provider could gain a web presence with access to an audience of long-haul visitors. In addition, because participating businesses were able to update the details they gave on a regular basis, the information provided remained accurate. And to maintain and improve standards, Tourism New Zealand organised a scheme whereby organisations appearing on the website underwent an independent evaluation against a set of agreed national standards of quality. As part of this, the effect of each business on the environment was considered.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: To communicate the New Zealand experience, the site also carried features relating to famous people and places. One of the most popular was an interview with former New Zealand All Blacks rugby captain Tana Umaga. Another feature that attracted a lot of attention was an interactive journey through a number of the locations chosen for blockbuster films which had made use of New Zealand's stunning scenery as a backdrop. As the site developed, additional features were added to help independent travellers devise their own customised itineraries. To make it easier to plan motoring holidays, the site catalogued the most popular driving routes in the country, highlighting different routes according to the season and indicating distances and times.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Later, a Travel Planner feature was added, which allowed visitors to click and 'bookmark' places or attractions they were interested in, and then view the results on a map. The Travel Planner offered suggested routes and public transport options between the chosen locations. There were also links to accommodation in the area. By registering with the website, users could save their Travel Plan and return to it later, or print it out to take on the visit. The website also had a 'Your Words' section where anyone could submit a blog of their New Zealand travels for possible inclusion on the website.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The Tourism New Zealand website won two Webby awards for online achievement and innovation. More importantly perhaps, the growth of tourism to New Zealand was impressive. Overall tourism expenditure increased by an average of 6.9% per year between 1999 and 2004. From Britain, visits to New Zealand grew at an average annual rate of 13% between 2002 and 2006, compared to a rate of 4% overall for British visits abroad.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The website was set up to allow both individuals and travel organisations to create itineraries and travel packages to suit their own needs and interests. On the website, visitors can search for activities not solely by geographical location, but also by the particular nature of the activity. This is important as research shows that activities are the key driver of visitor satisfaction, contributing 74% to visitor satisfaction, while transport and accommodation account for the remaining 26%. The more activities that visitors undertake, the more satisfied they will be. It has also been found that visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life. Many long-haul travellers enjoy such learning experiences, which provide them with stories to take home to their friends and family. In addition, it appears that visitors to New Zealand don't want to be 'one of the crowd' and find activities that involve only a few people more special and meaningful.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: It could be argued that New Zealand is not a typical destination. New Zealand is a small country with a visitor economy composed mainly of small businesses. It is generally perceived as a safe English-speaking country with a reliable transport infrastructure. Because of the long-haul flight, most visitors stay for longer (average 20 days) and want to see as much of the country as possible on what is often seen as a once-in-a-lifetime visit. However, the underlying lessons apply anywhere - the effectiveness of a strong brand, a strategy based on unique experiences and a comprehensive and user-friendly website.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-7
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the text in each gap.

content:
• easy for tourism-related businesses to get on the list
• allowed businesses to $795$information regularly
• provided a country-wide evaluation of businesses, including their impact on the $796$
• e.g. an interview with a former sports$797$, and an interactive tour of various locations used in$798$
• included a map showing selected places, details of public transport and local $800$
• travellers could send a link to their $801$

questions.analysis: 题干中的"allowed businesses to"对应原文中的"able to"，"information"对应原文中的"details"，"regularly"对应原文中的"on a regular basis"。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案是"update"，因为这是文章中描述企业可以定期更新信息的动词。

central_sentences.sentence: because participating businesses were able to update the details they gave on a regular basis, the information provided remained accurate.
central_sentences.translation: 由于参与的企业能够定期更新他们提供的详细信息，所提供的信息保持准确。

target_sen.target_sen: In addition, because participating businesses were able to update the details they gave on a regular basis, the information provided remained accurate.
In addition, because participating businesses were able to update the details they gave on a regular basis, the information provided remained accurate.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "environment"。题干中的 "evaluation" 和 "impact" 是关键线索词。在原文中，"evaluation" 对应 "evaluation"，"impact" 对应 "effect"。原文提到 "the effect of each business on the environment was considered"，这与题干中的 "including their impact on the 【 】" 对应。因此，正确答案是 "environment"。

central_sentences.sentence: As part of this, the effect of each business on the environment was considered.
central_sentences.translation: 作为评估的一部分，每个企业对环境的影响都被考虑在内。

target_sen.target_sen: As part of this, the effect of each business on the environment was considered.

And to maintain and improve standards, Tourism New Zealand organised a scheme whereby organisations appearing on the website underwent an independent evaluation against a set of agreed national standards of quality.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "captain"。题干中的 "interview with a former sports【 】" 对应文章中的 "interview with former New Zealand All Blacks rugby captain"。通过识别题干中的关键词 "interview", "former", 和 "sports"，可以在文章中找到对应的句子。虽然 "sports" 在文章中具体化为 "rugby"，但这并不影响理解。文章明确指出了 "captain" 是正确答案，而不是其他可能的词如 "player" 或 "coach"。

central_sentences.sentence: One of the most popular was an interview with former New Zealand All Blacks rugby captain Tana Umaga.
central_sentences.translation: 其中最受欢迎的是对前新西兰全黑队橄榄球队队长塔纳·乌马加的采访。

target_sen.target_sen: One of the most popular was an interview with former New Zealand All Blacks rugby captain Tana Umaga.
One of the most popular was an interview with former New Zealand All Blacks rugby captain Tana Umaga.
One of the most popular was an interview with former New Zealand All Blacks rugby captain Tana Umaga.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "films"。题干中的 "interactive tour of various locations used in" 对应原文中的 "interactive journey through a number of the locations chosen for blockbuster films"。原文明确指出这些地点是用于 "blockbuster films" 的拍摄，因此 "films" 是最准确的答案。学生需要通过识别同义表达和理解上下文来找到正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Another feature that attracted a lot of attention was an interactive journey through a number of the locations chosen for blockbuster films which had made use of New Zealand's stunning scenery as a backdrop.
central_sentences.translation: 另一个吸引了大量关注的特色是通过一系列选作大片拍摄地点的互动旅程，这些地点都利用了新西兰令人惊叹的自然风光作为背景。

target_sen.target_sen: Another feature that attracted a lot of attention was an interactive journey through a number of the locations chosen for blockbuster films which had made use of New Zealand's stunning scenery as a backdrop.

Another feature that attracted a lot of attention was an interactive journey through a number of the locations chosen for blockbuster films which had made use of New Zealand's stunning scenery as a backdrop.

questions.analysis: 答案是"season"。题目中的线索词"driving routes"和"varied depending on"在原文中分别对应"driving routes"和"highlighting different routes according to"。原文句子明确指出驾车路线根据季节变化，因此答案是"season"。

central_sentences.sentence: To make it easier to plan motoring holidays, the site catalogued the most popular driving routes in the country, highlighting different routes according to the season and indicating distances and times.
central_sentences.translation: 为了使规划自驾游更加方便，该网站对国内最受欢迎的驾车路线进行了分类，根据季节突出显示不同的路线，并标明距离和时间。

target_sen.target_sen: To make it easier to plan motoring holidays, the site catalogued the most popular driving routes in the country, highlighting different routes according to the season and indicating distances and times.

To make it easier to plan motoring holidays, the site catalogued the most popular driving routes in the country, highlighting different routes according to the season and indicating distances and times.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "accommodation"。题干中的 "map showing selected places, details of public transport and local 【 】" 对应原文中的 "The Travel Planner offered suggested routes and public transport options between the chosen locations. There were also links to accommodation in the area."。题干中的 "local" 对应原文中的 "in the area"，而 "details of public transport" 对应 "public transport options"。因此，最直接对应的答案是 "accommodation"。

central_sentences.sentence: The Travel Planner offered suggested routes and public transport options between the chosen locations. There were also links to accommodation in the area.

The Travel Planner offered suggested routes and public transport options between the chosen locations.
There were also links to accommodation in the area.

questions.analysis: 答案是“blog”，因为题干中的“travellers could send a link to their”与原文中的“anyone could submit a blog”意思相同。题干中的“Your Words”也在原文中明确提到。因此，最直接对应的答案是“blog”。

central_sentences.sentence: The website also had a 'Your Words'section where anyone could submit a blog of their New Zealand travels for possible inclusion on the website
central_sentences.translation: 该网站还有一个"你的话"栏目，任何人都可以提交他们在新西兰旅行的博客，有机会被收录到网站上。

groups.desc:
Questions 8-13
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The website www.newzealand.com aimed to provide ready-made itineraries and packages for travel companies and individual tourists.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "provide ready-made itineraries and packages" 表示网站提供现成的行程和套餐，而原文中的 "allow ... to create itineraries and travel packages" 表示网站允许用户自己创建行程和套餐。这是一个重要的区别，题干和原文表达的意思相反，因此正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The website was set up to allow both individuals and travel organisations to create itineraries and travel packages to suit their own needs and interests.
central_sentences.translation: 该网站的设立是为了让个人和旅游机构都能够创建符合自身需求和兴趣的行程和旅行套餐。

target_sen.target_sen: The website was set up to allow both individuals and travel organisations to create itineraries and travel packages to suit their own needs and interests.

questions.title: It was found that most visitors started searching on the website by geographical location.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“发现大多数游客在网站上是按地理位置开始搜索的”。然而，原文中并没有明确提到大多数游客是按地理位置开始搜索的，也没有提到大多数游客不是按地理位置开始搜索的。原文只是提到游客可以按地理位置或活动性质搜索，但没有说明哪种方式是大多数游客的首选。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有明确提到，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: On the website, visitors can search for activities not solely by geographical location, but also by the particular nature of the activity.
central_sentences.translation: 在网站上，游客不仅可以按地理位置搜索活动，还可以按活动的特定性质进行搜索。

target_sen.target_sen: On the website, visitors can search for activities not solely by geographical location, but also by the particular nature of the activity.
The more activities that visitors undertake, the more satisfied they will be.
It has also been found that visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life.
In addition, it appears that visitors to New Zealand don't want to be 'one of the crowd'and find activities that involve only a few people more special and meaningful.
On the website, visitors can search for activities not solely by geographical location, but also by the particular nature of the activity.

This is important as research shows that activities are the key driver of visitor satisfaction, contributing 74% to visitor satisfaction, while transport and accommodation account for the remaining 26%.
On the website, visitors can search for activities not solely by geographical location, but also by the particular nature of the activity.

questions.title: According to research, 26% of visitor satisfaction is related to their accommodation.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“26% of visitor satisfaction is related to their accommodation”，意思是游客满意度的26%与他们的住宿有关。然而，原文中明确指出“transport and accommodation account for the remaining 26%”，即交通和住宿共同占26%。因此，题干中的陈述是不准确的，因为26%是交通和住宿合起来的比例，而不是单单住宿的比例。因此，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: This is important as research shows that activities are the key driver of visitor satisfaction, contributing 74% to visitor satisfaction, while transport and accommodation account for the remaining 26%.
central_sentences.translation: 这一点很重要，因为研究表明，活动是游客满意度的主要驱动因素，对游客满意度的贡献率为74%，而交通和住宿则占剩余的26%。

target_sen.target_sen: This is important as research shows that activities are the key driver of visitor satisfaction, contributing 74% to visitor satisfaction, while transport and accommodation account for the remaining 26%.

This is important as research shows that activities are the key driver of visitor satisfaction, contributing 74% to visitor satisfaction, while transport and accommodation account for the remaining 26%.

This is important as research shows that activities are the key driver of visitor satisfaction, contributing 74% to visitor satisfaction, while transport and accommodation account for the remaining 26%.

questions.title: Visitors to New Zealand like to become involved in the local culture.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为题干中的 "Visitors to New Zealand like to become involved in the local culture" 与原文中的 "visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life" 是同义表达。题干中的 "like to become involved in the local culture" 对应原文中的 "enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive" 和 "traditional Maori life"。因此，题干中的信息在原文中得到了明确的支持。

central_sentences.sentence: It has also been found that visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life.
central_sentences.translation: 研究还发现，游客最喜欢的文化活动是那些互动性强的活动，比如参观毛利人的集会场所（marae）以了解传统毛利生活。

target_sen.target_sen: On the website, visitors can search for activities not solely by geographical location, but also by the particular nature of the activity.
The more activities that visitors undertake, the more satisfied they will be.
It has also been found that visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life.
In addition, it appears that visitors to New Zealand don't want to be 'one of the crowd'and find activities that involve only a few people more special and meaningful.
It has also been found that visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life.
It has also been found that visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life.
It has also been found that visitors enjoy cultural activities most when they are interactive, such as visiting a marae (meeting ground) to learn about traditional Maori life.

questions.title: Visitors like staying in small hotels in New Zealand rather than in larger ones.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“游客喜欢住在小酒店而不是大酒店”，而原文中并没有明确提到游客对小酒店和大酒店的偏好。虽然提到游客喜欢小规模的活动，但这并不能推断出他们更喜欢住在小酒店。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: New Zealand is a small country with a visitor economy composed mainly of small businesses.
central_sentences.translation: 新西兰是一个小国，其旅游经济主要由小型企业构成。

target_sen.target_sen: Because of the long-haul flight, most visitors stay for longer (average 20 days) and want to see as much of the country as possible on what is often seen as a once-in-a-lifetime visit.
New Zealand is a small country with a visitor economy composed mainly of small businesses.

questions.title: Many visitors feel it is unlikely that they will return to New Zealand after their visit.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE。题干中的 "unlikely" 和 "return" 在原文中对应的是 "once-in-a-lifetime visit"，这表明游客认为他们不太可能再来新西兰。题干中的 "many visitors" 对应的是 "most visitors"。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以确定题干信息在原文中得到了体现，因此答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Because of the long-haul flight, most visitors stay for longer (average 20 days) and want to see as much of the country as possible on what is often seen as a once-in-a-lifetime visit. However
central_sentences.translation: 由于长途飞行的原因，大多数游客会停留更长时间（平均20天），并希望尽可能多地看到这个国家的景色，因为这通常被视为一生一次的访问。

target_sen.target_sen: Because of the long-haul flight, most visitors stay for longer (average 20 days) and want to see as much of the country as possible on what is often seen as a once-in-a-lifetime visit.
Because of the long-haul flight, most visitors stay for longer (average 20 days) and want to see as much of the country as possible on what is often seen as a once-in-a-lifetime visit.

content.title: Why being bored is stimulating - and useful, too
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲了无聊的定义和特征，以及无聊可能的作用和功能。\n简短总结：无聊是一种难以定义的心理状态，可能包括其他情绪，如挫折、冷漠和抑郁，它可能是一种低能量、平淡的情绪，也可能是一种焦躁不安的情绪，它可能起到保护人们免受“有害”社交情境的作用。', 'B': '这段话主要讲了德国康斯坦茨大学的Thomas Goetz及其团队通过询问人们对无聊的经历，最近确定了五种不同类型的无聊：冷漠型、校准型、寻找型、反应型和冷漠型。这些可以在两个轴上绘制出来，一个从左到右，衡量低到高的兴奋度，另一个从上到下，衡量感觉的积极或消极程度。有趣的是，Goetz发现，虽然人们会经历各种各样的无聊，但他们往往专注于其中一种。在这五种类型中，最具破坏性的是“反应型”无聊，它具有高度兴奋和消极情绪的爆炸性组合。最有用的是Goetz所称的“冷漠型”无聊：某人没有参与任何令人满意的事情，但仍感到放松和平静。然而，目前尚不清楚是否有任何性格特征可以预测我们可能容易产生哪种类型的无聊。', 'C': '心理学家Sandi Mann在英国中央兰开夏大学进一步指出：“所有的情绪都有其存在的原因，包括无聊。”她发现无聊会让我们更有创造力。在去年发表的实验中，Mann发现，被要求抄写电话簿15分钟的人在如何使用聚苯乙烯杯子方面提出了更多创意，而对照组则没有。Mann得出结论，被动、无聊的活动对于创造力最有益，因为它让思维自由发散。事实上，她甚至建议我们在生活中寻找更多的无聊。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了心理学家John Eastwood对于无聊的看法，他认为心不在焉并不代表无聊，而无聊是一种不受欢迎的状态，尽管无聊可能有助于生存，但如果被放任不管，它仍然可能是有害的。无聊的核心特征是注意力系统的失灵，导致无法集中注意力，时间感觉过得非常缓慢，而努力改善情况可能会让人感觉更糟。最令人担忧的是，反复无法引起注意力可能会导致我们不再知道该做什么，也不再关心。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了研究人员正在探索为什么注意力系统会失效，他们认为其中一部分原因与个性有关，而且无聊易感性与多种特质有关联，对于那些易感到无聊的人来说，他们在教育、职业甚至生活中的前景都较差，但无聊本身并不能致命，我们需要在陷入危险之前采取措施来缓解它，研究人员建议通过“接近”无聊的方式来减轻无聊感，而不是通过吃零食、看电视或使用社交媒体等方式来转移注意力。', 'F': '心理学家Francoise Wemelsfelder推测，我们过度连接的生活方式甚至可能成为新的无聊源泉。因此，与其寻求更多的精神刺激，也许我们应该放下手机，利用无聊来激励我们以更有意义的方式参与世界。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: We all know how it feels - it's impossible to keep your mind on anything, time stretches out, and all the things you could do seem equally unlikely to make you feel better. But defining boredom so that it can be studied in the lab has proved difficult. For a start, it can include a lot of other mental states, such as frustration, apathy, depression and indifference. There isn't even agreement over whether boredom is always a low-energy, flat kind of emotion or whether feeling agitated and restless counts as boredom, too. In his book, Boredom: A Lively History, Peter Toohey at the University of Calgary, Canada, compares it to disgust - an emotion that motivates us to stay away from certain situations. 'If disgust protects humans from infection, boredom may protect them from "infectious" social situations,' he suggests.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: By asking people about their experiences of boredom, Thomas Goetz and his team at the University of Konstanz in Germany have recently identified five distinct types: indifferent, calibrating, searching, reactant and apathetic. These can be plotted on two axes - one running left to right, which measures low to high arousal, and the other from top to bottom, which measures how positive or negative the feeling is. Intriguingly, Goetz has found that while people experience all kinds of boredom, they tend to specialise in one. Of the five types, the most damaging is 'reactant' boredom with its explosive combination of high arousal and negative emotion. The most useful is what Goetz calls 'indifferent' boredom: someone isn't engaged in anything satisfying but still feels relaxed and calm. However, it remains to be seen whether there are any character traits that predict the kind of boredom each of us might be prone to.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Psychologist Sandi Mann at the University of Central Lancashire, UK, goes further. 'All emotions are there for a reason, including boredom,' she says. Mann has found that being bored makes us more creative. 'We're all afraid of being bored but in actual fact it can lead to all kinds of amazing things,' she says. In experiments published last year, Mann found that people who had been made to feel bored by copying numbers out of the phone book for 15 minutes came up with more creative ideas about how to use a polystyrene cup than a control group. Mann concluded that a passive, boring activity is best for creativity because it allows the mind to wander. In fact, she goes so far as to suggest that we should seek out more boredom in our lives.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Psychologist John Eastwood at York University in Toronto, Canada, isn't convinced. 'If you are in a state of mind-wandering you are not bored,' he says. 'In my view, by definition boredom is an undesirable state.' That doesn't necessarily mean that it isn't adaptive, he adds. 'Pain is adaptive - if we didn't have physical pain, bad things would happen to us. Does that mean that we should actively cause pain? No. But even if boredom has evolved to help us survive, it can still be toxic if allowed to fester.' For Eastwood, the central feature of boredom is a failure to put our 'attention system' into gear. This causes an inability to focus on anything, which makes time seem to go painfully slowly. What's more, your efforts to improve the situation can end up making you feel worse. 'People try to connect with the world and if they are not successful there's that frustration and irritability,' he says. Perhaps most worryingly, says Eastwood, repeatedly failing to engage attention can lead to a state where we don't know what to do any more, and no longer care.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Eastwood's team is now trying to explore why the attention system fails. It's early days but they think that at least some of it comes down to personality. Boredom proneness has been linked with a variety of traits. People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly. Other personality traits, such as curiosity, are associated with a high boredom threshold. More evidence that boredom has detrimental effects comes from studies of people who are more or less prone to boredom. It seems those who bore easily face poorer prospects in education, their career and even life in general. But of course, boredom itself cannot kill - it's the things we do to deal with it that may put us in danger. What can we do to alleviate it before it comes to that? Goetz's group has one suggestion. Working with teenagers, they found that those who 'approach' a boring situation - in other words, see that it's boring and get stuck in anyway - report less boredom than those who try to avoid it by using snacks, TV or social media for distraction.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Psychologist Francoise Wemelsfelder speculates that our over-connected lifestyles might even be a new source of boredom. 'In modern human society there is a lot of overstimulation but still a lot of problems finding meaning,' she says. So instead of seeking yet more mental stimulation, perhaps we should leave our phones alone, and use boredom to motivate us to engage with the world in a more meaningful way.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 14-19
The text has six paragraphs, A-F.
Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below and move it into the gap.

list.text: The productive outcomes that may result from boredom
What teachers can do to prevent boredom
A new explanation and a new cure for boredom
Problems with a scientific approach to boredom
A potential danger arising from boredom
Creating a system of classification for feelings of boredom
Age groups most affected by boredom
Identifying those most affected by boredom

questions.analysis: Section A主要讨论了科学研究无聊时遇到的困难。文章开头提到“defining boredom so that it can be studied in the lab has proved difficult”，这句话直接指出了科学研究无聊的困难。接着，文章进一步讨论了无聊的定义问题和不同情绪状态的混淆。这些内容都表明了科学研究无聊时遇到的问题。因此，Section A对应的小标题是iv: Problems with a scientific approach to boredom。

central_sentences.sentence: But defining boredom so that it can be studied in the lab has proved difficult.
central_sentences.translation: 但是为了在实验室中研究无聊，对无聊进行定义已经被证明是困难的。

target_sen.target_sen: But defining boredom so that it can be studied in the lab has proved difficult.
But defining boredom so that it can be studied in the lab has proved difficult.

questions.analysis: Section B的主要内容是Thomas Goetz和他的团队如何通过研究确定了五种不同类型的无聊，并且将这些类型绘制在两个轴上。这清楚地表明他们在创建一个关于无聊情绪的分类系统，这与小标题vi: Creating a system of classification for feelings of boredom直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: By asking people about their experiences of boredom, Thomas Goetz and his team at the University of Konstanz in Germany have recently identified five distinct types: indifferent, calibrating, searching, reactant and apathetic. These can be plotted on two axes - one running left to right, which measures low to high arousal, and the other from top to bottom, which measures how positive or negative the feeling is.
central_sentences.translation: 通过询问人们关于无聊的经历，德国康斯坦茨大学的Thomas Goetz和他的团队最近识别出了五种不同类型的无聊：冷漠型、校准型、搜索型、反应型和冷漠型。这些可以被绘制在两个轴上 - 一个从左到右，测量从低到高的唤醒程度，另一个从上到下，测量感觉的正面或负面程度。

target_sen.target_sen: By asking people about their experiences of boredom, Thomas Goetz and his team at the University of Konstanz in Germany have recently identified five distinct types: indifferent, calibrating, searching, reactant and apathetic.
These can be plotted on two axes - one running left to right, which measures low to high arousal, and the other from top to bottom, which measures how positive or negative the feeling is.

questions.analysis: Section C的主要内容是关于无聊的积极结果。心理学家Sandi Mann的研究表明，无聊可以使人变得更有创造力，并且可以带来各种惊人的事情。这与小标题i: The productive outcomes that may result from boredom直接对应，因为小标题中的“productive outcomes”与文章中的“more creative”和“lead to all kinds of amazing things”是同义表达。因此，选择i作为Section C的标题是最合适的。

central_sentences.sentence: Mann has found that being bored makes us more creative. 'We'reall afraid of being bored but in actual fact it can lead to all kinds of amazing things,' she says. In
central_sentences.translation: Mann发现无聊会使我们更有创造力。她说："我们都害怕感到无聊，但事实上它可以带来各种令人惊叹的结果。"

target_sen.target_sen: Mann has found that being bored makes us more creative.

In experiments published last year, Mann found that people who had been made to feel bored by copying numbers out of the phone book for 15 minutes came up with more creative ideas about how to use a polystyrene cup than a control group.

syn_ary.synonym: lead to all kinds of amazing things

target_sen.target_sen: 'We'reall afraid of being bored but in actual fact it can lead to all kinds of amazing things,' she says.
'All emotions are there for a reason, including boredom,'she says.
Mann has found that being bored makes us more creative.
'We'reall afraid of being bored but in actual fact it can lead to all kinds of amazing things,' she says.

In experiments published last year, Mann found that people who had been made to feel bored by copying numbers out of the phone book for 15 minutes came up with more creative ideas about how to use a polystyrene cup than a control group.
In fact, she goes so far as to suggest that we should seek out more boredom in our lives.
Mann concluded that a passive, boring activity is best for creativity because it allows the mind to wander.

questions.analysis: Section D主要讨论了无聊的潜在危险，特别是当无聊得不到解决时可能带来的负面影响。关键句子提到无聊可能是“toxic”（有害的），并且可能导致一种状态，即我们不知道该做什么，并且不再关心。这些表达与小标题v: A potential danger arising from boredom中的“potential danger”是同义的。因此，Section D对应的小标题是v。

central_sentences.sentence: That doesn't necessarily mean that it isn't adaptive, he adds. 'Pain is adaptive - if we didn't have physical pain, bad things would happen to us. Does that mean that we should actively cause pain? No. But even if boredom has evolved to help us survive, it can still be toxic if allowed to fester.'
central_sentences.translation: 这并不意味着它就没有适应性，他补充道。"疼痛是有适应性的——如果我们没有身体上的疼痛，坏事就会发生在我们身上。这是否意味着我们应该主动造成疼痛？不。但即使无聊已经进化成帮助我们生存的机制，如果任其恶化，它仍然可能变得有害。"

target_sen.target_sen: But even if boredom has evolved to help us survive, it can still be toxic if allowed to fester.'

questions.analysis: Section E 主要讨论了哪些人最容易受到无聊的影响。通过描述不同性格特征与无聊的关系，文章识别了容易感到无聊的人群。这与标题 viii: Identifying those most affected by boredom 完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Boredom proneness has been linked with a variety of traits. People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly. Other personality traits, such as curiosity, are associated with a high boredom threshold.
central_sentences.translation: 容易感到无聊与多种性格特征有关。那些被快乐驱动的人似乎特别容易受到影响。其他性格特征，如好奇心，则与较高的无聊阈值相关。

target_sen.target_sen: Boredom proneness has been linked with a variety of traits.
Other personality traits, such as curiosity, are associated with a high boredom threshold.
Boredom proneness has been linked with a variety of traits.
People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly.
It seems those who bore easily face poorer prospects in education, their career and even life in general.

questions.analysis: Section F的主要内容是讨论一种新的解释（过度连接的生活方式可能是无聊的新来源）和新的解决方法（利用无聊来激励我们以更有意义的方式与世界互动）。因此，最合适的小标题是iii: A new explanation and a new cure for boredom。

central_sentences.sentence: Psychologist Francoise Wemelsfelder speculates that our over-connected lifestyles might even be a new source of boredom.
central_sentences.translation: 心理学家Francoise Wemelsfelder推测，我们过度连接的生活方式甚至可能是导致无聊的一个新原因。

central_sentences.sentence: So instead of seeking yet more mental stimulation, perhaps we should leave our phones alone, and use boredom to motivate us to engage with the world in a more meaningful way.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，与其寻求更多的精神刺激，也许我们应该放下手机，利用无聊来激励我们以更有意义的方式与世界互动。

target_sen.target_sen: Psychologist Francoise Wemelsfelder speculates that our over-connected lifestyles might even be a new source of boredom.
Psychologist Francoise Wemelsfelder speculates that our over-connected lifestyles might even be a new source of boredom.

target_sen.target_sen: So instead of seeking yet more mental stimulation, perhaps we should leave our phones alone, and use boredom to motivate us to engage with the world in a more meaningful way.
So instead of seeking yet more mental stimulation, perhaps we should leave our phones alone, and use boredom to motivate us to engage with the world in a more meaningful way.

groups.title: List of Ideas
groups.desc:
Questions 20-23

Look at the following people (Questions 20-23) and the list of ideas below.
Match each person with the correct idea, A-E.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: The way we live today may encourage boredom.
One sort of boredom is worse than all the others.
Levels of boredom may fall in the future.
Trying to cope with boredom can increase its negative effects.
Boredom may encourage us to avoid an unpleasant experience.

questions.title: Peter Toohey
questions.analysis: Peter Toohey的观点是无聊可以保护我们远离某些不愉快的社交场合，这与选项E“Boredom may encourage us to avoid an unpleasant experience”完全一致。文章中使用了“stay away from certain situations”和“'infectious' social situations”来表达“avoid unpleasant experience”的意思。

central_sentences.sentence: 'If disgust protects humans from infection, boredom may protect them from 'infectious' social situations,' he suggests.
central_sentences.translation: 他建议，"如果厌恶保护人类免受感染，那么无聊可能会保护他们免受'传染性'的社交场合。"

target_sen.target_sen: In his book, Boredom: A Lively History, Peter Toohey at the University of Calgary, Canada, compares it to disgust - an emotion that motivates us to stay away from certain situations.

questions.title: Thomas Goetz
questions.analysis: Thomas Goetz的研究发现了五种不同类型的无聊，并指出其中“反应性”无聊是最具破坏性的。这与选项B“one sort of boredom is worse than all the others”直接对应，因此选项B是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Of the five types, the most damaging is 'reactant'boredom with its explosive combination of high arousal and negative emotion. The
central_sentences.translation: 在这五种类型中，最具破坏性的是"反应性"无聊，它是高唤醒和负面情绪的爆炸性组合。

target_sen.target_sen: By asking people about their experiences of boredom, Thomas Goetz and his team at the University of Konstanz in Germany have recently identified five distinct types: indifferent, calibrating, searching, reactant and apathetic.

questions.title: John Eastwood
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D。文章中John Eastwood的观点是，试图应对无聊的努力可能会增加其负面影响。具体来说，他提到“your efforts to improve the situation can end up making you feel worse”，这与选项D“Trying to cope with boredom can increase its negative effects”直接对应。因此，D是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: What's more, your efforts to improve the situation can end up making you feel worse.
central_sentences.translation: 更重要的是，你为改善情况所做的努力可能最终会让你感觉更糟。

target_sen.target_sen: Psychologist John Eastwood at York University in Toronto, Canada, isn't convinced.

questions.title: Francoise Wemelsfelder

central_sentences.sentence: Psychologist Francoise Wemelsfelder speculates that our over-connected lifestyles might even be a new source of boredom.

groups.title: Responses to boredom
groups.desc:
Questions 24-26
Complete the summary below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the text in each gap.
groups.collect: For John Eastwood, the central feature of boredom is that people cannot $818$ , due to a failure in what he calls the 'attention system', and as a result they become frustrated and irritable. His team suggests that those for whom $819$is an important aim in life may have problems in coping with boredom, whereas those who have the characteristic of $820$ can generally cope with it.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "central feature" 对应原文中的 "central feature"，"failure" 对应 "failure"，"attention system" 对应 "attention system"，"cannot" 对应 "inability to"。通过这些对应关系，可以定位到原文中的句子 "This causes an inability to focus on anything"，其中 "focus" 是对应答案的位置。因此，正确答案是 "focus"。

central_sentences.sentence: For Eastwood, the central feature of boredom is a failure to put our 'attention system'into gear. This causes an inability to focus on anything, which makes time seem to go painfully slowly. What
central_sentences.translation: 对于伊斯特伍德来说，无聊的核心特征是我们无法启动"注意力系统"。这导致无法集中注意力在任何事物上，使得时间似乎过得异常缓慢。

target_sen.target_sen: For Eastwood, the central feature of boredom is a failure to put our 'attention system'into gear.
For Eastwood, the central feature of boredom is a failure to put our 'attention system'into gear.
'People try to connect with the world and if they are not successful there's that frustration and irritability,'he says.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“important aim in life”对应文章中的“motivated by”，而“problems in coping with boredom”对应文章中的“suffer particularly badly”。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确认答案所在的句子是“People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly.”。因此，正确答案是“pleasure”。

central_sentences.sentence: People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly.
central_sentences.translation: 那些以快乐为动机的人似乎特别容易受到无聊的困扰。

target_sen.target_sen: People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly.
People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "problems in coping with boredom" 对应原文中的 "suffer particularly badly"，而 "characteristic of curiosity" 对应原文中的 "curiosity are associated with a high boredom threshold"。这表明好奇心是能够帮助应对无聊的特质，因此答案是 "curiosity"。

central_sentences.sentence: Other personality traits, such as curiosity, are associated with a high boredom threshold.
central_sentences.translation: 其他人格特质，比如好奇心，与高无聊忍受阈值相关。

target_sen.target_sen: People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly.
Other personality traits, such as curiosity, are associated with a high boredom threshold.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The Painting Fool is one of a growing number of computer programs which, so their makers claim, possess creative talents. Classical music by an artificial composer has had audiences enraptured, and even tricked them into believing a human was behind the score. Artworks painted by a robot have sold for thousands of dollars and been hung in prestigious galleries. And software has been built which creates art that could not have been imagined by the programmer.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Human beings are the only species to perform sophisticated creative acts regularly. If we can break this process down into computer code, where does that leave human creativity? 'This is a question at the very core of humanity,' says Geraint Wiggins, a computational creativity researcher at Goldsmiths, University of London. 'It scares a lot of people. They are worried that it is taking something special away from what it means to be human.'

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: To some extent, we are all familiar with computerised art. The question is: where does the work of the artist stop and the creativity of the computer begin? Consider one of the oldest machine artists, Aaron, a robot that has had paintings exhibited in London's Tate Modern and the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art. Aaron can pick up a paintbrush and paint on canvas on its own. Impressive perhaps, but it is still little more than a tool to realise the programmer's own creative ideas.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Simon Colton, the designer of the Painting Fool, is keen to make sure his creation doesn't attract the same criticism. Unlike earlier 'artists' such as Aaron, the Painting Fool only needs minimal direction and can come up with its own concepts by going online for material. The software runs its own web searches and trawls through social media sites. It is now beginning to display a kind of imagination too, creating pictures from scratch. One of its original works is a series of fuzzy landscapes, depicting trees and sky. While some might say they have a mechanical look, Colton argues that such reactions arise from people's double standards towards software-produced and human-produced art. After all, he says, consider that the Painting Fool painted the landscapes without referring to a photo. 'If a child painted a new scene from its head, you'd say it has a certain level of imagination,' he points out. The same should be true of a machine.' Software bugs can also lead to unexpected results. Some of the Painting Fool's paintings of a chair came out in black and white, thanks to a technical glitch. This gives the work an eerie, ghostlike quality. Human artists like the renowned Ellsworth Kelly are lauded for limiting their colour palette - so why should computers be any different?

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Researchers like Colton don't believe it is right to measure machine creativity directly to that of humans who 'have had millennia to develop our skills'. Others, though, are fascinated by the prospect that a computer might create something as original and subtle as our best artists. So far, only one has come close. Composer David Cope invented a program called Experiments in Musical Intelligence, or EMI. Not only did EMI create compositions in Cope's style, but also that of the most revered classical composers, including Bach, Chopin and Mozart. Audiences were moved to tears, and EMI even fooled classical music experts into thinking they were hearing genuine Bach. Not everyone was impressed however. Some, such as Wiggins, have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience, and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked. Meanwhile, Douglas Hofstadter of Indiana University said EMI created replicas which still rely completely on the original artist's creative impulses. When audiences found out the truth they were often outraged with Cope, and one music lover even tried to punch him. Amid such controversy, Cope destroyed EMI's vital databases.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: But why did so many people love the music, yet recoil when they discovered how it was composed? A study by computer scientist David Moffat of Glasgow Caledonian University provides a clue. He asked both expert musicians and non-experts to assess six compositions. The participants weren't told beforehand whether the tunes were composed by humans or computers, but were asked to guess, and then rate how much they liked each one. People who thought the composer was a computer tended to dislike the piece more than those who believed it was human. This was true even among the experts, who might have been expected to be more objective in their analyses.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Where does this prejudice come from? Paul Bloom of Yale University has a suggestion: he reckons part of the pleasure we get from art stems from the creative process behind the work. This can give it an 'irresistible essence', says Bloom. Meanwhile, experiments by Justin Kruger of New York University have shown that people's enjoyment of an artwork increases if they think more time and effort was needed to create it. Similarly, Colton thinks that when people experience art, they wonder what the artist might have been thinking or what the artist is trying to tell them. It seems obvious, therefore, that with computers producing art, this speculation is cut short - there's nothing to explore. But as technology becomes increasingly complex, finding those greater depths in computer art could become possible. This is precisely why Colton asks the Painting Fool to tap into online social networks for its inspiration: hopefully this way it will choose themes that will already be meaningful to us.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-31

Choose the correct answer, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: What is the writer suggesting about computer-produced works in the first paragraph?

options.text: People's acceptance of them can vary considerably.
A great deal of progress has already been attained in this field.
They have had more success in some artistic genres than in others.
The advances are not as significant as the public believes them to be.

questions.analysis: 文章的第一段描述了计算机程序在艺术创作领域取得的显著成就，包括音乐和绘画方面的成功。这些成就表明该领域已经取得了很大的进展，因此选项B是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: The Painting Fool is one of a growing number of computer programs which, so their makers claim, possess creative talents.
central_sentences.translation: 绘画傻瓜是越来越多的计算机程序之一，据其制作者声称，这些程序具有创造性天赋。

target_sen.target_sen: The Painting Fool is one of a growing number of computer programs which, so their makers claim, possess creative talents.

questions.title: According to Geraint Wiggins, why are many people worried by computer art?

options.text: It is aesthetically inferior to human art
It may ultimately supersede human art.
It undermines a fundamental human quality.
It will lead to a deterioration in human ability.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为文章中Geraint Wiggins明确表示，很多人担心电脑艺术会“taking something special away from what it means to be human”，这与选项C中的“undermines a fundamental human quality”意思相同。这个担忧是关于电脑艺术削弱了人类的基本品质，而不是其他选项所提到的美学、取代或能力下降的问题。

central_sentences.sentence: This is a question at the very core of humanity,'says Geraint Wiggins, a computational creativity researcher at Goldsmiths, University of London. 'It scares a lot of people. They are worried that it is taking something special away from what it means to be human
central_sentences.translation: Geraint Wiggins，伦敦大学金史密斯学院的计算创造力研究员说："这是一个关乎人性核心的问题。它吓到了很多人。他们担心它正在夺走一些作为人类特有的东西。"

target_sen.target_sen: 'It scares a lot of people.
They are worried that it is taking something special away from what it means to be human.'
'This is a question at the very core of humanity,'says Geraint Wiggins, a computational creativity researcher at Goldsmiths, University of London.

the technical standard of its output

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C: the source of its subject matter。文章中明确指出，Aaron依赖于程序员的创意，而Painting Fool可以自己从网上获取素材并产生自己的概念。这是两者之间的关键区别。

central_sentences.sentence: Unlike earlier 'artists'such as Aaron, the Painting Fool only needs minimal direction and can come up with its own concepts by going online for material. The
central_sentences.translation: 与早期的"艺术家"如Aaron不同，绘画傻瓜只需要最少的指导，就能通过上网获取素材来提出自己的概念。

target_sen.target_sen: Unlike earlier 'artists'such as Aaron, the Painting Fool only needs minimal direction and can come up with its own concepts by going online for material.
Unlike earlier 'artists'such as Aaron, the Painting Fool only needs minimal direction and can come up with its own concepts by going online for material.
Simon Colton, the designer of the Painting Fool, is keen to make sure his creation doesn't attract the same criticism.
Unlike earlier 'artists'such as Aaron, the Painting Fool only needs minimal direction and can come up with its own concepts by going online for material.
After all, he says, consider that the Painting Fool painted the landscapes without referring to a photo.
Some of the Painting Fool's paintings of a chair came out in black and white, thanks to a technical glitch.

questions.title: What point does Simon Colton make in the fourth paragraph?

options.text: Software-produced art is often dismissed as childish and simplistic.
The same concepts of creativity should not be applied to all forms of art.
It is unreasonable to expect a machine to be as imaginative as a human being.
People tend to judge computer art and human art according to different criteria.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D。文章中明确提到“Colton argues that such reactions arise from people's double standards towards software-produced and human-produced art.”，这句话中的“double standards”与题干中的“different criteria”是同义表达，说明人们对电脑艺术和人类艺术的评价标准是不同的。因此，答案是D。

central_sentences.sentence: Colton argues that such reactions arise from people's double standards towards software-produced and human-produced art.
central_sentences.translation: 科尔顿认为，这些反应源于人们对软件创作和人类创作的艺术持有双重标准。

questions.title: The writer refers to the paintings of a chair as an example of computer art which

options.text: exhibits a certain level of genuine artistic skill.
closely resembles that of a well-known artist.
highlights the technical limitations of the software.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到，由于技术故障，Painting Fool画的椅子是黑白的，这给作品带来了“eerie, ghostlike quality”（怪异、幽灵般的效果）。这与选项A中的“achieves a particularly striking effect”（达到了特别引人注目的效果）是同义表达。因此，正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: Some of the Painting Fool's paintings of a chair came out in black and white, thanks to a technical glitch. This gives the work an eerie, ghostlike quality.
central_sentences.translation: 由于技术故障，Painting Fool的一些椅子画作呈现出黑白效果。这给作品带来了一种怪异、幽灵般的质感。

syn_ary.synonym: Painting Fool's paintings of a chair

target_sen.target_sen: Some of the Painting Fool's paintings of a chair came out in black and white, thanks to a technical glitch.
Simon Colton, the designer of the Painting Fool, is keen to make sure his creation doesn't attract the same criticism.
Unlike earlier 'artists'such as Aaron, the Painting Fool only needs minimal direction and can come up with its own concepts by going online for material.
After all, he says, consider that the Painting Fool painted the landscapes without referring to a photo.
Some of the Painting Fool's paintings of a chair came out in black and white, thanks to a technical glitch.

groups.title: List of Ideas
groups.desc:
Questions 32-37

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-G below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: generating work that was virtually indistinguishable from that of humans.
knowing whether it was the work of humans or software.
producing work entirely dependent on the imagination of its creator.
comparing the artistic achievements of humans and computers.
revealing the technical details of his program.
persuading the public to appreciate computer art.
discovering that it was the product of a computer program.

questions.title: Simon Colton says it is important to consider the long-term view when
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D，因为题干中的“long-term view”在原文中对应的是“millennia to develop our skills”，而Simon Colton的观点是不能直接比较机器和人类的艺术成就，这正是答案D所表达的意思。

central_sentences.sentence: Researchers like Colton don't believe it is right to measure machine creativity directly to that of humans who 'have had millennia to develop our skills'.
central_sentences.translation: 像科尔顿这样的研究人员认为，直接将机器的创造力与"已经花费了千年时间发展技能"的人类相比较是不恰当的。

target_sen.target_sen: Researchers like Colton don't believe it is right to measure machine creativity directly to that of humans who 'have had millennia to develop our skills'.

questions.title: David Cope's EMI software surprised people by
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A。文章中提到EMI软件创作的音乐作品让人们误以为是人类作曲家创作的，甚至欺骗了古典音乐专家。这与选项A的描述“生成的作品几乎与人类的作品无法区分”是同义表达。因此，选项A是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: EMI even fooled classical music experts into thinking they were hearing genuine Bach.
central_sentences.translation: EMI甚至欺骗了古典音乐专家，让他们以为他们听到的是真正的巴赫作品。

target_sen.target_sen: Audiences were moved to tears, and EMI even fooled classical music experts into thinking they were hearing genuine Bach.
Composer David Cope invented a program called Experiments in Musical Intelligence, or EMI.

questions.title: Geraint Wiggins criticised Cope for not
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是E，因为题干问的是Wiggins批评Cope的原因，而文章中明确提到Wiggins批评Cope是因为他故意模糊解释软件的工作原理，这与E选项中的“revealing the technical details of his program”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Some, such as Wiggins, have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience, and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked.
central_sentences.translation: 一些人，比如维金斯，抨击科普的作品为伪科学，并谴责他故意含糊其辞地解释软件的工作原理。

target_sen.target_sen: Some, such as Wiggins, have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience, and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked.
Some, such as Wiggins, have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience, and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked.
Some, such as Wiggins, have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience, and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked.
Composer David Cope invented a program called Experiments in Musical Intelligence, or EMI.
Not only did EMI create compositions in Cope's style, but also that of the most revered classical composers, including Bach, Chopin and Mozart.
Some, such as Wiggins, have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience, and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked.
When audiences found out the truth they were often outraged with Cope, and one music lover even tried to punch him.
Amid such controversy, Cope destroyed EMI's vital databases.

questions.title: Douglas Hofstadter claimed that EMI was
questions.analysis: Douglas Hofstadter 认为 EMI 创作的作品完全依赖于原始艺术家的创意冲动，这与选项 C "producing work entirely dependent on the imagination of its creator" 完全一致。因此，正确答案是 C。

central_sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, Douglas Hofstadter of Indiana University said EMI created replicas which still rely completely on the original artist's creative impulses.
central_sentences.translation: 与此同时，印第安纳大学的道格拉斯·霍夫施塔特表示，EMI创造的作品完全依赖于原创艺术家的创意冲动。

target_sen.target_sen: Meanwhile, Douglas Hofstadter of Indiana University said EMI created replicas which still rely completely on the original artist's creative impulses.
Composer David Cope invented a program called Experiments in Musical Intelligence, or EMI.
Not only did EMI create compositions in Cope's style, but also that of the most revered classical composers, including Bach, Chopin and Mozart.
Audiences were moved to tears, and EMI even fooled classical music experts into thinking they were hearing genuine Bach.
Meanwhile, Douglas Hofstadter of Indiana University said EMI created replicas which still rely completely on the original artist's creative impulses.
Amid such controversy, Cope destroyed EMI's vital databases.

questions.title: Audiences who had listened to EMI's music became angry after
questions.analysis: 题干中的“became angry after”在原文中对应的表达是“outraged with Cope, and one music lover even tried to punch him”，而“after”对应的是“when”，并且“discovered”对应的是“found out”。这些线索词和原文中的同义替换词明确指出了听众在发现音乐是由计算机程序创作的真相后感到愤怒。因此，答案是G: discovering that it was the product of a computer program。

central_sentences.sentence: When audiences found out the truth they were often outraged with Cope, and one music lover even tried to punch him.
central_sentences.translation: 当听众发现真相时，他们经常对Cope感到愤怒，一位音乐爱好者甚至试图打他。

target_sen.target_sen: When audiences found out the truth they were often outraged with Cope, and one music lover even tried to punch him.
When audiences found out the truth they were often outraged with Cope, and one music lover even tried to punch him.

questions.title: The participants in David Moffat's study had to assess music without
questions.analysis: 题干要求描述David Moffat的研究条件，文章中明确提到参与者在评估音乐时不知道这些音乐是由人还是计算机创作的。选项B "knowing whether it was the work of humans or software" 与文章中的描述完全一致，因此是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The participants weren't told beforehand whether the tunes were composed by humans or computers, but were asked to guess, and then rate how much they liked each one.
central_sentences.translation: 参与者事先并不知道这些曲子是由人类还是计算机创作的，但被要求猜测，然后对每首曲子的喜爱程度进行评分。

target_sen.target_sen: He asked both expert musicians and non-experts to assess six compositions.
The participants weren't told beforehand whether the tunes were composed by humans or computers, but were asked to guess, and then rate how much they liked each one.

syn_ary.synonym: study by computer scientist David Moffat

target_sen.target_sen: A study by computer scientist David Moffat of Glasgow Caledonian University provides a clue.

groups.desc:
Questions 38-40

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?

Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: Moffat's research may help explain people's reactions to EMI.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Moffat's research may help explain people's reactions to EMI”与原文中的“study by computer scientist David Moffat... provides a clue”直接对应。题干提到Moffat的研究可能解释人们对EMI的反应，而原文明确指出Moffat的研究提供了一个线索来解释人们对音乐的反应。因此，题干中的陈述在文章中得到了支持，答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: But why did so many people love the music, yet recoil when they discovered how it was composed? A study by computer scientist David Moffat of Glasgow Caledonian University provides a clue.
central_sentences.translation: 但为什么这么多人喜欢这音乐，却在发现它的创作方式后退缩了呢？格拉斯哥卡利多尼亚大学的计算机科学家David Moffat的一项研究提供了线索。

syn_ary.synonym: study by computer scientist David Moffat

target_sen.target_sen: A study by computer scientist David Moffat of Glasgow Caledonian University provides a clue.
A study by computer scientist David Moffat of Glasgow Caledonian University provides a clue.

questions.title: The non-experts in Moffat's study all responded in a predictable way.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“非专家在Moffat的研究中都以可预测的方式回应”。在原文中，虽然提到了非专家的反应，但并没有明确说明这些反应是否是可预测的。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的对应，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: He asked both expert musicians and non-experts to assess six compositions. The participants weren't told beforehand whether the tunes were composed by humans or computers, but were asked to guess, and then rate how much they liked each one.
central_sentences.translation: 他要求专业音乐家和非专业人士评估六首作品。参与者事先不知道这些曲子是由人类还是计算机创作的，但被要求猜测，然后评价他们有多喜欢每一首。

target_sen.target_sen: He asked both expert musicians and non-experts to assess six compositions.

questions.title: Justin Kruger's findings cast doubt on Paul Bloom's theory about people's prejudice towards computer art.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Justin Kruger's findings cast doubt on Paul Bloom's theory about people's prejudice towards computer art”意思是“Justin Kruger 的发现质疑了 Paul Bloom 关于人们对电脑艺术偏见的理论”。通过对比文章内容可以发现，Kruger 的实验结果实际上支持了 Bloom 的理论，因为 Kruger 的实验表明人们更喜欢那些他们认为花费了更多时间和努力的艺术作品，这与 Bloom 的观点一致。因此，题干中的陈述是错误的，正确答案是“NO”。

central_sentences.sentence: a suggestion: he reckons part of the pleasure we get from art stems from the creative process behind the work. This can give it an 'irresistible essence', says Bloom. Meanwhile, experiments by Justin Kruger of New York University have shown that people's enjoyment of an artwork increases if they think more time and effort was needed to create it
central_sentences.translation: 耶鲁大学的Paul Bloom提出了一个观点：他认为我们从艺术中获得的部分愉悦来自于创作过程。同时，纽约大学的Justin Kruger的实验表明，如果人们认为一件艺术品需要更多的时间和努力来创作，他们对该艺术品的喜爱程度就会增加。

syn_ary.synonym: experiments by Justin Kruger of New York University have shown

target_sen.target_sen: Meanwhile, experiments by Justin Kruger of New York University have shown that people's enjoyment of an artwork increases if they think more time and effort was needed to create it.

syn_ary.synonym: Paul Bloom of Yale University has a suggestion

target_sen.target_sen: Paul Bloom of Yale University has a suggestion: he reckons part of the pleasure we get from art stems from the creative process behind the work.
